DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is adopted as follows:

Defendant the Regents of the University of California's demurrer to plaintiff's second cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to more clearly allege the nature of the claimed violation of Health and Safety Code section 1317. (See, e.g., Jackson v. East Bay Hospital (9th Cir. 2001) 246 F.3d 1248, 1259-1260 [the duty of reasonable care under 1317(c) applies in two situations, one of which is when "the hospital does not provide a medical screening, examination, or evaluation to determine if the patient presents an emergency medical condition," which "constitutes 'a refusal to render emergency services and care'"; the second applies "when a doctor diagnoses a condition, but declines to provide treatment because he determines either that the condition is not an 'emergency medical condition' or that the hospital does not have the appropriate facilities or personnel to provide care"]; compare id. at 1263-1264 (Tashima, C.J., conc. in part and dis. in part [disagreeing with majority's interpretation of 1317 and finding genuine issues of material fact as to whether the hospital exercised reasonable care in its screening and evaluation procedures]; George v. Sonoma County Sheriff's Dept. (N.D. Cal. 2010) 732 F.Supp.2d 922, 945 [finding a triable issue of fact existed as to the "care [and] treatment ... necessary to relieve or eliminate the emergency medical condition" diagnosed by Sutter physicians under 1317.1(a)(1)].)

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
